skill.name: standup-meeting
skill.description: Conduct effective daily standup meetings for agile teams. Use it to structure updates, surface blockers, and keep team synchronization focused.
skill.summary: Facilitate concise daily standups, async updates, blocker tracking, and follow-up action items.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and blocker findings were false positives caused by Markdown fences, agile terminology, and tag text in SKILL.md. The only retained risk factor is low-risk network exposure from two external reference links, with no evidence of automatic requests, scripts, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable code.

low_findings.title: External Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill includes two hardcoded links to public agile reference pages. They are not invoked by code and do not transmit user data, but users may leave the marketplace when opening them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain plain Markdown links only. There is no script, network client, or automated request behavior around these URLs.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences and inline tag formatting. No executable command, shell invocation, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is Markdown syntax or inline tag text. The file contains instructions and examples, not runnable shell or Ruby code.

low_findings.title: Blocker Static Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance alerts are term matches in descriptive agile content. No cryptographic function, network scanner, probe command, or prompt injection attempt was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are the skill description, standup headings, and a reference link. None contains executable logic or security-sensitive operations.

content.value_statement: Daily standups can drift into status reports or long problem-solving sessions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured formats for concise updates, blockers, and action items.
actual_capabilities: Provides a three-question daily standup format for yesterday, today, and blockers.
Supports board-based standups that review work by status and sprint progress.
Creates async standup templates for remote teams and Slack-style updates.
Structures standup minutes with attendees, sprint context, summary, blockers, and actions.
Reinforces time-boxing, participation, parking-lot follow-up, and focused facilitation.
limitations: Does not connect to Jira, Slack, GitHub, or calendar systems automatically.
Does not verify sprint data, story points, attendance, or blocker ownership.
Does not replace human facilitation for conflict resolution or prioritization.
Includes general agile guidance, not organization-specific process policy.

use_cases.title: Keep Sprint Teams Synchronized
use_cases.description: Run a daily standup that captures completed work, planned work, blockers, and clear follow-up actions.

use_cases.title: Collect Remote Team Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare an async update template that remote contributors can complete before the team sync window.

use_cases.title: Review Work From the Board
use_cases.description: Walk the sprint board by status to focus on flow, blocked items, review needs, and sprint progress.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Standup Agenda
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-minute daily standup agenda for a team of [team size]. Include yesterday, today, blockers, and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Updates Into Minutes
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these standup notes into clear minutes with summary, individual updates, blockers, and owners: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Async Standup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an async standup template for a distributed team working on [sprint goal]. Include progress, blockers, and follow-up fields.

prompt_templates.title: Facilitate a Board Walk
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this board snapshot to run a board-based standup. Prioritize blocked, in progress, review, and done items: [paste board].

output_examples.input: A five-person team needs a daily standup format for Sprint 10.
output_examples.output: A time-boxed agenda with attendee count, sprint day, three update questions, blocker capture, and assigned action items.

output_examples.input: Remote contributors need to post daily progress before core hours.
output_examples.output: An async update template with yesterday, today, blockers, sprint progress, and follow-up ownership fields.

output_examples.input: The team wants to walk the board instead of giving individual status reports.
output_examples.output: A board-based facilitation outline that reviews in-progress work, blocked cards, review needs, completed items, and sprint health.

best_practices: Keep the standup time-boxed and move detailed problem solving to a parking lot.
Capture every blocker with an owner, next step, and expected follow-up time.
Use the same format consistently so updates remain short and comparable.
anti_patterns: Using the standup as a management status report instead of a team planning sync.
Letting technical debates consume the meeting instead of scheduling follow-up discussions.
Leaving blockers unassigned or undocumented after the meeting ends.

faq.question: Can this skill run a daily scrum for a software team?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides daily scrum structures for updates, blockers, sprint progress, and action items.

faq.question: Does it integrate with project management tools?
faq.answer: No. It creates templates and meeting outputs, but it does not connect to external systems.

faq.question: Can it support async standups?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a remote-team template suitable for Slack-style daily updates.

faq.question: Can it summarize messy standup notes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize pasted notes into summary, updates, blockers, and follow-up actions.

faq.question: Is it only for Scrum teams?
faq.answer: No. Scrum teams are the clearest fit, but any team can use the update and blocker formats.

faq.question: Does it make sprint decisions automatically?
faq.answer: No. It supports facilitation and documentation, but humans decide priority, scope, and ownership.
